LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANTONY RILEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT ANTONY RILEY'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MUZHAN SHAHRESTANI IS OVERRULED. PER WELL-SETTLED DEMURRER AND BREACH OF CONTRACT RULES, THE COURT'S TASK ON THIS DEMURRER IS TO DETERMINE WHETHER, IN LIGHT OF THE ASSERTED EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE, THE GUARANTY AGREEMENT IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO MS. SHAHRESTANI'S PROFFERED INTERPRETATIONS OF THAT AGREEMENT. IT IS. LIBERALLY CONSTRUING THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT AND CONSIDERING ALL OF THOSE ALLEGATIONS AND THE DOCUMENTS ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT, THE GUARANTY AGREEMENT IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO BOTH OF THE FOLLOWING INTERPRETATIONS: 1) THE GUARANTY AGREEMENT COVERS THE INITIAL INVESTMENT MADE BY MS. SHAHRESTANI AS REFLECTED IN THE SHAREHOLDER AGREEMENT AND 2) THE GUARANTY AGREEMENT COVERS THE ENTIRETY OF THE INVESTMENT MADE BY MS. SHAHRESTANI UP TO BUT NOT EXCEEDING $170,000. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Svetlana Rishna, Tel. No. 1-510-299-5346 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Martin D. Goodman, Tel. No. 1-415-397-7956 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Gregory Rougeau of the Brunetti Rogeau LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-992-8940 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANTONY RILEY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant Antony Riley's demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract in the complaint filed by plaintiff Muzhan Shahrestani is overruled. Per well-settled demurrer and breach of contract rules, the court's task on this demurrer is to determine whether, in light of the asserted extrinsic evidence, the guaranty agreement is reasonably susceptible to Ms. Shahrestani's proffered interpretations of that agreement. It is. Liberally construing the allegations in the complaint and considering all of those allegations and the documents attached to the complaint, the guaranty agreement is reasonably susceptible to both of the following interpretations: 1) the guaranty agreement covers the initial investment made by Ms. Shahrestani as reflected in the shareholder agreement and 2) the guaranty agreement covers the entirety of the investment made by Ms. Shahrestani up to but not exceeding $170,000. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
